error.signature: Error: Could not connect to Redis at 127.0.0.1:6379: Connection refused

verdict.summary: Redis server not running or not listening on expected port.

dead_ends.why_fails: Connection refused means the service is not running, not a broken installation

dead_ends.action: Change the port in redis.conf to avoid conflict
dead_ends.why_fails: Other applications expect the default port. Fix the port conflict instead.

workarounds.how: sudo systemctl start redis && sudo systemctl enable redis

workarounds.action: Check if Redis is running and on which port
workarounds.how: redis-cli ping  # should return PONG; ss -tlnp | grep redis

workarounds.action: Check redis.conf bind and protected-mode settings
workarounds.how: bind 127.0.0.1; protected-mode yes  # default; change for remote access
